titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Living Theatre Records
titlestmt.author: by Leigh Golden and Jennifer Meehan

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday October 2, 2024 at 03:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of general files, correspondence, subject files, publicity files, scripts, journals, writings, personal papers, photographs, audiovisual material, and born digital materials relating to The Living Theatre, its founders, Julian Beck and Judith Malina, and its co-director, Hanon Reznikov. The collection documents the adminstration of the theater, its stage productions, and its relationship to other avant-garde and radical cultural and political movements in the United States and Europe during the time period from the 1960s to the present. Also included are extensive diaries and journals of Judith Malina and Julian Beck, as well as their personal papers and writings. The photographs and audio-visual material largely document specific productions.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Morgan & Rosenthal on the Alfred Z. Baker, Jr. Fund and Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2008, and from Boo-Hooray on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund and the Adele Gutman Nathan Theatrical Collection Fund, 2013.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: In-process material. This collection is closed to researchers until processing is completed. For further information, consult Access Services.

Series I. Personal and Family Papers; Subseries. Judith Malina's diaries, notebooks and calendars are open for research. As additional series and subseries are processed, they will be opened to researchers.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Living Theatre Records is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Living Theatre Records. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

bioghist.head: The Living Theatre
bioghist.p: The Living Theatre was founded in 1947 by Julian Beck and Judith Malina in New York City. The company performed politically charged plays commenting on war, Wall Street, and income inequality even being arrested during some of their public performances. The group produced experimental and little-known plays by writers including Gertrude Stein, William Carlos Williams, and John Ashbery. In 1961, the company toured Europe with one of its biggest successes, "The Connection" and other plays including Paradise Now and The Legacy of Cain . The Living Theatre left New York in 1964 after failing to pay $23,000 in back taxes. Malina and Beck represented themselves in the trial. The theatre company focused on avant garde productions including The Connection and The Brig . Theatre productions often broke the fourth wall and included audience participation. Theatre company members were also arrested for various acts of civil disobedience. The theatre company performed all over the world in public and unique spaces. The theatre returned to New York City in the 1980s. After Julian Beck's death in 1985, Hanon Reznikov was named codirector. Reznikov died in 2008 and Malina in 2015. The Living Theatre continues with artistic director Brad Burgess to conduct workshops and perform in the United States and abroad.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of general files, correspondence, subject files, publicity files, scripts, journals, writings, personal papers, photographs, audiovisual material, and born digital materials relating to The Living Theatre, its founders, Julian Beck and Judith Malina, and its co-director, Hanon Reznikov. The collection documents the adminstration of the theater, its stage productions, and its relationship to other avant-garde and radical cultural and political movements in the United States and Europe during the time period from the 1960s to the present. Also included are extensive diaries and journals of Judith Malina and Julian Beck, as well as their personal papers and writings. The photographs and audio-visual material largely document specific productions.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into one series. I. Personal and Family Papers.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This finding aid will be revised as additional parts of the collection are processed and revisions in arrangement or description are made by staff.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The subseries is arranged into three groupings: I. Diaries, II. Appointment Calendars, III. Notebooks. Materials are arranged chronologically within each grouping.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Judith Malina was a prolific diarist and kept an almost daily diary for over 70 years, documenting both her personal and professional lives in great detail. Malina wrote about daily events in her diaries and excluded almost no details. She comments on her physical and mental state, her sexual activities, and her family history. She writes about conversations, feelings, relationships, and events both in her personal life and in the wider world. Malina also wrote about her family, including her parents, her life with Julian Beck, her husband from 1947 until his death in 1985 and co-creator of The Living Theatre, her children, as well as her life with Hanon Reznikov, co-director of The Living Theatre who she married in 1988.

The diaries are a comprehensive look into a life full of teaching, directing, acting, research, and activism. They track the formation of The Living Theatre and its growth and performances over time. Malina's diaries portray her desire to form the theatre, the theatre's triumphs and challenges over the years, and its transition into the 21st century. They include details about productions, performances, rehearsing, and writing. The diaries also offer a glimpse into the lives of the artists, actors, writers, and directors that were colleagues, friends, collaborators, and students of Malina's.

Judith Malina's diaries are mostly handwritten in notebooks of various sizes, and some include small photographs, ephemera, and portions of correspondence or conversations written in Malina's hand. Most of the diaries were identified as being written in New York City, but other locations where Malina traveled to, lived in, and worked in include Brazil, France, Italy, Germany, Switzerland, and Greece. Grove Press published her diaries in one volume entitled The Diaries of Judith Malina, 1947-1957 in 1984. In addition to the diaries, the subseries includes appointment calendars and notebooks in which Malina kept her notes about different productions and research topics.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: In the subseries inventory, diaries are titled according to the location at which they were written. Diaries for which a location was not specified are titled, "Diary with unspecified location."

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Judith Malina was born in Kiel, Germany on June 4, 1926. In 1929, her family emigrated to New York City. She met her future husband and collaborator, Julian Beck in 1943. In 1945, Malina made her acting debut, studying at the New School for Social Research, studying with Erwin Piscator. She married Julian Beck in 1948. Their son, Garrick Beck, was born in 1949. Her daughter Isha was born in 1967. Together in 1947, they founded The Living Theatre, an alternative theater company which was based in New York City. She created, directed, and acted in the company's plays including Antigone , Frankenstein , and Paradise Now . She also taught at New York University and Columbia University. Malina appeared in film and television, including The Sopranos , The Addams Family , and Dog Day Afternoon . Along with articles and monographs, she published her early diaries and a memoir of her theatre training under Erwin Piscator, The Piscator Notebook and a book of poetry, Poems of a Wandering Jewess . In 1988, Malina married Hanon Reznikov, a writer, director, actor in the Living Theatre, who became codirector of the company after Julian Beck's death. Reznikov died in 2008. Malina died on April 10, 2015.

Drawings; Greek and Hebrew writings, etc.
"Dr. & Analy," notes on dreams and emotional state
Chronological list of events in Judith Malina's life and Living Theatre repertory; brief biogaphy of Judith Malina and miscellaneous notes
"Notes and casts for Greek readings; word games, notes for Marrying Maiden "
Notes on Theatre of Chance, Marrying Maiden , Greek play readings, etc.
Notes on Worldwide Strike for Peace, etc.
Partial script for Man is Man by Bertolt Brecht with stage directions
Blank notepad in cover containing photos, notes, receipts
Third-person narrative about experiences during European tour
"Antigone notes, FS notes and personal notes"
"Meditation on Possible Forms" and other poems
Notes on The Legacy of Cain and discussion in Brazil
Notes on "some things to do" and "some things to write"
"2 small poems & some misc. notes on lecture tour"
Notes on The Legacy of Cain
Blank notebook with inscription to Judith Malina from Stephan Schulberg

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To Tara Kennedy on 10/15/19 for mold removal

did.unittitle: Address book containing notes and childhood memoirs
" Zero Method : NYC & N.M. NY Times headlines read onstage Dec 17.91-Oct 9.93"
Empty notebook containing a pressed flower
Lines for unidentified performance in French
Lists and notes; letter to Lester; poems and verse fragments
"Notes for an Anarchist Analysis of " [various quotes regarding war]
Notes on Orpheus, Greek myths, rhetoric, etc.
Notes regarding medical care of [Julian Beck?] and miscellaneous notes
Stories in the tradition of Yiddish folktales
Small ring binder containing alphabetical dividers with arithmetic calculations
